$schema_comment: A complete design profile. design-analyst returns a spec referencing tokens defined here; main agent generates code using only those tokens.
one_line: Warm-neutral palette with serif display + sans body. Feels like Apple, Stripe, Anthropic, or v0's example pages.

background.role: Page background. Warm cream, never pure white.

surface.role: Elevated surfaces (cards, modals). One step brighter than bg.

surface_subtle.role: Inset surfaces (input fields when not focused, code blocks).

text_primary.role: Body text, headlines. Near-black with warmth, never pure #000.

text_muted.role: De-emphasized text. Last resort — prefer text_secondary.

border.role: Subtle dividers, input borders. Always low-contrast.

border_emphasis.role: Strong borders (focused input, primary button outline).

accent.role: Terracotta. Single accent. Use for links, small flourishes — NOT buttons.

accent_alt.role: Sage. Alternative accent for hybrid projects. Pick ONE — accent or accent_alt — never both.

rules: Pick ONE accent color per project — accent OR accent_alt, never both
Never use pure black (#000) for text — use #1A1818 with warmth
Never use pure white (#FFF) for the page background — use #FAF8F4. Pure white is for surfaces only
Accent is for LINKS and small accents. The primary button is BLACK on cream, not accent-colored
No gradients anywhere. None. Anti-pattern #1 for this profile

display.family: Tiempos Headline, Playfair Display, PT Serif Pro, Georgia, serif

mono.family: iA Writer Quattro Mono, JetBrains Mono, Menlo, monospace

rules: Display font is for h1-h3 ONLY. h4+ uses body font in heavier weight
Body text in serif feels like a 2010 blog — DON'T do it. Body is always Inter/sans
Hero headlines: display font, weight 500-600, size 4xl-6xl, letter-spacing -0.02em
Body line-height: 1.6 for paragraphs, 1.4 for UI labels, 1.2 for headlines
Tabular numbers (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums) for any aligned numeric columns
Generous whitespace is the look. Default to 1.5× what feels right
Between major sections: 96-128px vertical
Between paragraphs: 24px
Between form fields: 24px (not 16px — too tight for this aesthetic)
Default radius: md (6px). Editorial designs use SMALL radii, not pill shapes
Inputs: md (6px). Buttons: md (6px) — same as inputs
Never radius-full on rectangular buttons. Pill buttons feel SaaS, wrong here
Avatars and circular elements use radius-full obviously
Almost no shadows. This profile is FLAT.
If a shadow is absolutely needed (floating menu, modal), use: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,24,0.04), 0 1px 1px rgba(26,24,24,0.02)
Never use shadow as decoration. If you reach for one, ask: 'does this need elevation, or am I trying to make it pop?' If the latter, remove the shadow and rethink

button_primary.rule: Primary button is BLACK on cream. No gradient, no accent color.

input_text.rule: Focus ring is the BORDER going darker, not a separate ring. No glow effects. No orange browser-default ring — set :focus-visible to override

link.underline: always for inline links, on-hover for nav links
link.rule: Links are the ONLY place accent color appears in body content

login.structure: Left panel: form (centered vertically, ~40% width). Right panel: editorial quote with subtle grid background (~60% width). One testimonial, named person + title. No carousel of logos.
do: Single accent in the brand mark
OAuth buttons BELOW email (after primary path), not above
dont: Stacking marketing copy + form on the same side
'Demo credentials' boxes — feels like fintech bro

landing_hero.structure: Single-column, 720px max-width, generous top whitespace (160px+). Display headline in serif, supporting paragraph in sans. ONE primary CTA, optional secondary ghost button.
do: Real product imagery or none at all
Single-sentence value prop, not a list
dont: 3-up feature grid in the hero — push that below the fold
Hero video unless it's the actual product, not stock motion

dashboard_or_app_ui.structure: This profile is for marketing/editorial surfaces. For product UIs, switch to linear-product.
dashboard_or_app_ui.fallback: If forced, use generous spacing scaled down to 2/3 and keep the typography but expect the result to feel 'marketing-y'. Better: use linear-product.

animations.library: gsap (optional — most motion here is plain CSS transitions)
animations.philosophy: Restrained. Confidence is stillness. Subtle 200-300ms ease-out transitions on hover; 400-500ms fade-in on hero text. No scroll-jacking, no parallax.

patterns.hero_intro: Fade-up the headline 400ms ease-out, then fade-up the subhead at +150ms, then the CTA at +300ms. That's the entire hero animation.
patterns.hover: Background opacity shift or color tween, 200ms. NEVER scale transforms on layout elements (causes shift).
patterns.page_in: Optional 200ms fade-in on body content on first load.

tailwind_config_snippet.$comment: Drop-in extension. Main agent should write this to tailwind.config.js (or @theme in v4) and reference ONLY these tokens in components.
